assertions.title: Ofsted 9 Quality Standards checklist renders with evidence linking

steps.action: Open /childcore-a-home/ofsted and inspect the rendered standards.
steps.expected: All 9 Ofsted Quality Standards render with explicit compliance states and can be expanded from the dashboard.
steps.observed: The dashboard rendered 9 unique standard links, but each card showed percentage scores (for example 0% or 20%) instead of compliant / partially compliant / non-compliant labels.

steps.action: Open the Care Planning standard from the dashboard.
steps.expected: Clicking a standard expands or reveals linked evidence items with timestamps and authors.
steps.observed: Clicking Regulation 14 navigated to a separate 'Standard Evidence' page that showed only one evidence row ('new' with a description and Fully Evidenced badge). No timestamp, author, or inline dashboard expansion was present.

evidence.network: Shared dev-server log shows GET /childcore-a-home/ofsted -> 200, GET /childcore-a-home/ofsted/standards/9571d42f-56c9-4aa2-a7d3-aaa9a8b77cf7 -> 200, and POST /childcore-a-home/ofsted/standards/9571d42f-56c9-4aa2-a7d3-aaa9a8b77cf7 -> 200 while the standard detail loaded. DOM inspection counted 9 unique standard IDs on the dashboard.

assertions.issues: The dashboard does render the nine standards, but it does not expose the required compliant/partially-compliant/non-compliant states or an expanded evidence view with timestamps and authors.

assertions.title: Staff can link an existing record as evidence against a Quality Standard

steps.action: Open the existing care-plan detail page for Ava Validation.
steps.expected: The record detail page should expose a 'Link to Quality Standard' control and show any linked standards on the record itself.
steps.observed: The care-plan page showed Version history, Export PDF, Edit plan, and Submit for review actions, but no 'Link to Quality Standard' control or linkage badge anywhere on the record.

steps.action: Open the existing Regulation 14 detail page and inspect the available linking workflow.
steps.expected: If linking is supported, the UI should link from the record detail and remain bidirectional.
steps.observed: A 'Link Evidence' modal exists only on the standard-detail page. It accepts a raw Record ID and description, but the originating care-plan record still shows no standard badge or backlink after the evidence is present under the standard.

evidence.network: Shared dev-server log shows GET /childcore-a-home/persons/dad70c1e-640f-47cb-a2d7-4418649829fe/care-plans/83f6867a-43a2-4054-aff0-58cfb578b869 -> 200 and GET/POST /childcore-a-home/ofsted/standards/9571d42f-56c9-4aa2-a7d3-aaa9a8b77cf7 -> 200. No request originated from the care-plan detail page to link a Quality Standard because no such control is rendered there.

assertions.issues: Evidence can only be entered from the standard page via a generic Record ID modal; the record detail page lacks the required linking action and the bidirectional linkage badge.

assertions.title: Children's register records admission and departure per Schedule 4

steps.action: Open /childcore-a-home/ofsted/register and inspect the register state.
steps.expected: The register should show admitted children with Schedule 4 fields and support departure updates.
steps.observed: The page showed '0 current residents · 0 total entries' and the empty-state message 'No children registered yet.'

steps.action: Inspect the page controls for adding entries or filtering current vs departed children.
steps.expected: The UI should expose append-only entry management plus current/departed filters.
steps.observed: DOM inspection found no buttons, selects, or inputs on the page, so there is no UI to add an admission, record a departure, or filter current vs departed records.

evidence.network: Shared dev-server log shows GET /childcore-a-home/ofsted/register -> 200. The rendered page exposed no form or filter controls.

assertions.issues: The children's register route is present but only renders an empty state with no user-facing admission, departure, or filter workflow.

assertions.title: Each child's legal status is recorded and tracked with change history

steps.action: Open Ava Validation's LAC page at /childcore-a-home/persons/dad70c1e-640f-47cb-a2d7-4418649829fe/lac.
steps.expected: The child profile should show a current legal status in the header, plus access to status history and editing.
steps.observed: The page only showed a 'No LAC record' empty state with Placement plans / New LAC record actions; no legal status header or history was visible.

steps.action: Follow the visible New LAC record route directly.
steps.expected: A working creation/edit form should allow legal status capture, later changes, and audit history generation.
steps.observed: Opening /childcore-a-home/persons/dad70c1e-640f-47cb-a2d7-4418649829fe/lac/new returned a 404 page, so no legal-status form, change-history surface, or exportable record exists in the real UI.

evidence.network: Shared dev-server log shows GET /childcore-a-home/persons/dad70c1e-640f-47cb-a2d7-4418649829fe/lac -> 200 and repeated GET /childcore-a-home/persons/dad70c1e-640f-47cb-a2d7-4418649829fe/lac/new -> 404.

assertions.issues: The legal-status workflow is not testable through the real UI because the LAC overview is empty and the creation route returns 404.

assertions.title: Social worker and IRO contact details stored and accessible from child profile

steps.action: Inspect Ava Validation's LAC page on desktop.
steps.expected: The child profile should show social worker and IRO contact cards plus change/audit history.
steps.observed: The desktop page showed only the LAC empty state and no contact cards, audit trail entries, or contact actions.

steps.action: Reopen the same page in an iPhone 14 viewport.
steps.expected: The mobile profile should expose tap-to-call / tap-to-email actions from the contact card.
steps.observed: The mobile view still showed only the empty state with New LAC record / Create LAC record links and no social worker or IRO contact card.

evidence.network: Shared dev-server log shows GET /childcore-a-home/persons/dad70c1e-640f-47cb-a2d7-4418649829fe/lac -> 200 and GET /childcore-a-home/persons/dad70c1e-640f-47cb-a2d7-4418649829fe/lac/new -> 404. Because the contact-record creation route is missing, no contact or audit mutation request can be issued from the real UI.

assertions.issues: No social worker or IRO contact surface is rendered on the child profile, and the missing LAC creation route prevents populating or changing those details.

assertions.title: Placement plan created within 5 working days, with review cycle tracking

steps.action: Open Ava Validation's placement-plan list at /childcore-a-home/persons/dad70c1e-640f-47cb-a2d7-4418649829fe/lac/placement-plans.
steps.expected: The page should allow creation of a placement plan with the required domain sections and review-cycle tracking.
steps.observed: The route loaded, but only showed 'No placement plans yet' plus a Create placement plan link. No existing plan, countdown, review reminder, or overdue alert was visible.

steps.action: Follow the Create placement plan action.
steps.expected: A working placement-plan form should open so the plan can be created within the 5-working-day window.
steps.observed: The create route changed to /lac/placement-plans/new, but that URL resolves to a non-functional blank 404 surface with no interactive elements.

steps.action: Inspect the organisation dashboard for the promised placement-plan deadline/countdown signal.
steps.expected: The manager dashboard should show a countdown or alert for plans due within 5 working days and overdue reviews.
steps.observed: The dashboard text contained no placement-plan countdown, due-by notice, or overdue-review alert; it remained the generic widget placeholder dashboard.

evidence.network: Shared dev-server log shows GET /childcore-a-home/persons/dad70c1e-640f-47cb-a2d7-4418649829fe/lac/placement-plans -> 200, repeated GET /childcore-a-home/persons/dad70c1e-640f-47cb-a2d7-4418649829fe/lac/placement-plans/new -> 404, and GET /childcore-a-home/dashboard -> 200 with no placement-plan-specific follow-up requests.

assertions.issues: The placement-plan list exists only as an empty state, the creation route is missing, and the dashboard does not expose the required 5-working-day countdown or review alerts.

frictions.description: This retry reused an existing namespace account and organisation, but the prior password was unknown.
frictions.resolution: Used the real Forgot Password flow, recovered the reset link from the shared dev-server log, and signed back in as childcore-a-owner@example.test before validating the assigned assertions.

frictions.description: The in-app links to the missing /lac/new and /lac/placement-plans/new routes did not always replace the visible page content immediately during client-side navigation.
frictions.resolution: Opened the target URLs directly to capture the actual 404 states for the missing LAC and placement-plan creation surfaces.

blockers.description: The LAC creation surface is missing: /childcore-a-home/persons/dad70c1e-640f-47cb-a2d7-4418649829fe/lac/new returns 404.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tried the visible 'New LAC record' and 'Create LAC record' links from the LAC overview, then opened the route directly; every attempt resolved to the missing route.

blockers.description: The placement-plan creation surface is missing: /childcore-a-home/persons/dad70c1e-640f-47cb-a2d7-4418649829fe/lac/placement-plans/new returns 404.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Used the visible 'Create placement plan' CTA on the empty-state page and then opened the route directly; both paths ended at the same non-functional 404 surface.

summary: Tested 6 assertions: 0 passed, 6 failed. The Ofsted dashboard renders nine standards but does not show the required evidence metadata/expansion behavior, evidence linking is not available from record detail pages, the children's register is read-only empty state only, and the LAC plus placement-plan creation routes are missing from the real browser UI.
